message:
feat(pthread): port __pthread_cond_init/destroy/timedwait + __pthread_attr_init/destroy aliases (bd-vwrz8)

Adds the remaining glibc reserved-namespace aliases for the
pthread condvar lifecycle and thread-attribute lifecycle:

- `__pthread_cond_init` (delegates to `pthread_cond_init`)
- `__pthread_cond_destroy` (delegates to `pthread_cond_destroy`)
- `__pthread_cond_timedwait` (delegates to `pthread_cond_timedwait`)
- `__pthread_attr_init` (delegates to `pthread_attr_init`)
- `__pthread_attr_destroy` (delegates to `pthread_attr_destroy`)

These complete the underscored-alias coverage for the condvar
(init/destroy/wait/timedwait/signal/broadcast) and thread-
attribute (init/destroy) families that libstdc++ thread machinery
links against.

Implementation: thin no_mangle Rust extern "C" wrappers
delegating to the public names — same pattern as the previously-
shipped __pthread_create/join/detach/exit and
__pthread_cond_wait/signal/broadcast aliases.

Tests: 4 abi integration tests covering: (a) cond_init+destroy
round-trip; (b) cond_timedwait with abstime in the past returns
ETIMEDOUT immediately; (c) attr_init+destroy round-trip;
(d) attr_destroy on a zero-initialized (never-managed) attr
returns EINVAL, matching the public counterpart.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -815,7 +815,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-hmwxy","title":"PORT: fmtcheck (NetBSD/FreeBSD libutil printf-format compatibility check)","description":"NetBSD/FreeBSD libutil fmtcheck(user, default) returns user if its printf-format conversion specifiers are compatible with default; otherwise returns default. "Compatible" means same number of conversions AND same type-class for each (INT vs FP vs STR vs PTR vs N), with length modifiers normalized so that "%d" and "%i" interchange but "%d" vs "%ld" do NOT. Used by syslog wrappers and any tool that wants to safely accept a user-supplied format string as long as it does not change the variadic-argument shape. Currently missing entirely from frankenlibc-abi. Ship: pure-safe core::stdlib::fmtcheck::compatible(user, default) -> bool that delegates to the existing parse_format_string + walks the FormatSpec list mapping (conversion, length) to a normalized ConvShape enum + thin abi shim that returns user-pointer or default-pointer per the BSD contract.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T05:00:26.442450279Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T05:05:11.154259826Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T05:05:11.152360179Z","close_reason":"shipped: core::stdlib::fmtcheck (ConvShape + LenClass + compatible) + stdlib_abi::fmtcheck shim; 23+12 tests; clippy-clean; rch-built","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Verified with targeted fuzz-crate check/clippy, harness test, and workspace check/clippy/fmt via rch.  {"id":"bd-ho3il","title":"[LOW] review: fprintf executor matrix lags snprintf coverage","description":"[LOW] review: fprintf executor matrix lags snprintf coverage

Recent printf pipeline changes expanded the snprintf/sprintf executor matrix, including `NullPtr` and several 3/4/5-argument combinations, but the fprintf path remains much narrower.

Current gap:
- `crates/frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs:2712-2849` handles `PrintfArg::NullPtr` and multiple 3+/4+/5-argument combinations for `run_impl_snprintf` / `run_host_snprintf`.
- `crates/frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs:2854-2910` still only supports the small fprintf subset and returns `unsupported fprintf arg combination` for anything wider.

That creates a coverage skew inside the same executor family: newly-added fixture cases can pass through snprintf/sprintf while equivalent fprintf cases are silently untestable.

Review ask:
- keep the fprintf dispatch matrix in lockstep with the snprintf/sprintf executor surface
- add at least the currently-supported `NullPtr` and 3+/4+/5-argument combinations so harness coverage does not regress by API shape","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:29:44.457254443Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T22:35:40.067098287Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T22:35:40.064806561Z","close_reason":"Verified parity: run_impl_snprintf/run_host_snprintf and run_impl_fprintf/run_host_fprintf matchers in crates/frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs now carry identical arg-shape sets (17 match arms each; normalized diff shows EQUAL for both impl and host runners). NullPtr + 3/4/5-argument combinations present on both sides. Verified by:
  awk '/^fn run_impl_snprintf/,/^fn run_host_snprintf/' ... | grep -cE '^\s*\['  -> 17
  awk '/^fn run_impl_fprintf/,/^fn run_host_fprintf/' ... | grep -cE '^\s*\['    -> 17
  normalized sort/diff of arm shapes: EQUAL for both impl and host pairs.
End-to-end: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_harness cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test printf_conformance_conformance_test -> 14/0 passed in 2.08s. Work landed in commits 1c5a9d0f + 9ea2ad56.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","coverage","printf","review"]}
-{"id":"bd-huug1","title":"PORT: __pthread_cond_wait/signal/broadcast + __pthread_kill + __pthread_key_delete (glibc reserved aliases)","description":"Port the remaining glibc reserved-namespace aliases for the pthread condvar / signal / TSD entry points: __pthread_cond_wait, __pthread_cond_signal, __pthread_cond_broadcast, __pthread_kill, __pthread_key_delete. glibc emits these underscored variants both as internal-use entry points and as the symbols libstdc++ links against for std::condition_variable, std::call_once thread cancellation cleanup, and TSD destructors. Implementation: thin no_mangle Rust extern "C" wrappers that delegate to the public pthread_cond_wait/signal/broadcast, pthread_kill, pthread_key_delete in pthread_abi. Tests verify each alias produces the same observable behavior: condvar wake-up unblocks a waiting thread, kill delivers the signal to a target tid (we observe via pthread_sigmask + sigpending), and key_delete on a freshly-created key returns 0.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T11:34:10.301291885Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T11:34:10.301291885Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Implementation: thin no_mangle Rust extern "C" wrappers that delegate to the public pthread_cond_wait/signal/broadcast, pthread_kill, pthread_key_delete in pthread_abi. Tests verify each alias produces the same observable behavior: condvar wake-up unblocks a waiting thread, kill delivers the signal to a target tid (we observe via pthread_sigmask + sigpending), and key_delete on a freshly-created key returns 0.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T11:34:10.301291885Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T11:38:05.188998931Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T11:38:05.187000125Z","close_reason":"shipped: __pthread_cond_wait/signal/broadcast + __pthread_kill + __pthread_key_delete glibc reserved-namespace aliases delegating to existing public-name impls + 5 abi tests including condvar wake-up smoke tests, all green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-hxfu0","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] execvp_via_execve walks unbounded user-supplied `file` (bd-z4k96 class)","description":"FOUND + FIXED via review round 5 (commit 1d68447a).

execvp_via_execve at L62 dereferenced the caller's `file` pointer via CStr::from_ptr with no length bound. A non-NUL-terminated pathname (attacker-controlled or memory-corrupted) walked arbitrary process memory.

Reachability: every program that resolves a command via PATH (shell builtins, system(), subprocess wrappers, container runtime exec helpers) hits this path with caller-supplied text.

Fix: read_bounded_cstr replacement; on None return -1 + ENOENT.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T07:45:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T07:45:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T07:45:00Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commits 0c0b59f1 / 1d68447a / fe3f1c06; 3120/3120 abi tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bd-z4k96-class","high","review","unbounded-cstr"]}
 {"id":"bd-hzkc6","title":"syscall: signalfd4 existing fd update with same mask should return same fd without touching errno","description":"Host glibc returns the same fd and leaves errno unchanged for signalfd(existing_fd, &mask, 0). Add focused ABI regression coverage for the in-place update contract.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T10:56:03.717758642Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T05:06:51.635816783Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T05:06:51.633658460Z","close_reason":"signalfd4(existing_fd, same_mask, 0) → same_fd (in-place update) pinned.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-i18zl","title":"PORT: strvis + strnvis + strunvis + strnunvis (NetBSD vis(3) family)","description":"NetBSD vis(3) family — visual byte encoding/decoding used by mtree, fpr, cd9660, pkgsrc, BSD pkg tools and many ports for safely round-tripping arbitrary binary data through ASCII-only channels (file names in tar/cpio listings, log lines). Ship the four most commonly used variants: strvis(dst, src, flags) encodes src into dst (NUL terminated) returning the number of bytes written; strnvis(dst, dlen, src, flags) is the bounded variant returning -1 if dst too small; strunvis(dst, src) and strnunvis(dst, dlen, src) decode the inverse. Default mode encodes non-printable bytes as \\^X (control) or \\M-X (high-bit) per BSD vis(3); with VIS_OCTAL flag (0x01) all non-printable bytes go to \\NNN. Other VIS_* flags ignored in v1. Currently missing entirely from frankenlibc-abi. Ship: pure-safe core::stdio::vis::{encode_byte, decode_one, strvis, strunvis} + abi shims that handle raw-pointer NUL and bounds.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T06:33:44.856900442Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T06:42:13.077049471Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T06:42:13.075032533Z","close_reason":"shipped: core::stdio::vis (encode_byte/decode_one + recursive M- handling) + stdio_abi strvis/strnvis/strunvis/strnunvis; 21+12 tests; clippy-clean; rch-built","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
@@ -1181,6 +1181,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-voxzi","title":"PORT: strnstr (BSD bounded substring search)","description":"BSD strnstr(haystack, needle, n) — like strstr but searches at most n bytes of haystack, returning a pointer to the first occurrence of needle (still NUL-terminated) or NULL if not found. Used by many BSD ports; without it programs that depend on it will not link against frankenlibc. Currently missing from frankenlibc-abi/string_abi.rs. Ship core::string::str::strnstr (returning Option<usize>) plus an abi shim that handles NULL pointer and casts to char*.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-25T15:14:29.383398661Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T15:18:35.591905897Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T15:18:35.590011542Z","close_reason":"shipped: core::string::str::strnstr + string_abi::strnstr; 12+10 tests; bound-respecting via core_strnlen; clippy-clean","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-vq1g","title":"D.1: (SUPERSEDED) — folded into parent bd-9dq5","description":"## Goal
Implement the request-tracking infrastructure for async DNS and the gai_error/gai_cancel functions.

## Design

### Request Registry
A global mutex-protected HashMap<usize, AsyncDnsRequest> maps gaicb pointer addresses to request state. The gaicb struct (defined by POSIX) contains:
- ar_name: hostname to resolve
- ar_service: service name/port
- ar_request: hints (addrinfo struct)
- ar_result: output addrinfo list (written on completion)

### gai_error(req: *mut gaicb) -> c_int
1. Look up req address in registry
2. If not found: return EAI_SYSTEM (unknown request)
3. Return state: EAI_INPROGRESS (still running), 0 (completed successfully), EAI_CANCELLED, or the error code from getaddrinfo

### gai_cancel(req: *mut gaicb) -> c_int
1. Look up req in registry
2. If not found: return EAI_NOCANCEL
3. If already completed: return EAI_ALLDONE
4. If in progress: set CANCELLED flag, return EAI_CANCELLED (or EAI_NOTCANCELED if thread already started resolution)

### State Constants
- EAI_INPROGRESS = -100 (glibc value)
- EAI_CANCELLED = -101
- EAI_ALLDONE = -103
- EAI_NOTCANCELED = -102

## Implementation
File: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs (near existing gai_* functions)
- Add AsyncDnsRequest struct
- Add static ASYNC_DNS_REGISTRY: LazyLock<Mutex<HashMap<usize, AsyncDnsRequest>>>
- Replace gai_error body
- Replace gai_cancel body

## Testing
- gai_error on unknown request returns EAI_SYSTEM
- gai_cancel on unknown request returns EAI_NOCANCEL
- Register a fake completed request, gai_error returns 0
- Register a fake in-progress request, gai_cancel returns EAI_CANCELLED

## Acceptance Criteria
State machine is correct. Registry tracks requests. Tests pass.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:19:44.987634072Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T01:59:38.338086567Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T01:59:38.337881268Z","close_reason":"Superseded: Track D redesigned as synchronous-only. No request registry or futex wait needed. All logic fits in parent bd-9dq5.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["async","resolv","stub-elimination"]}
 {"id":"bd-vqevs","title":"PORT: sys_signame (BSD short signal-name array)","description":"Port the BSD `const char *const sys_signame[NSIG]` array — short uppercase signal names without the "SIG" prefix, indexed by signal number. Entries 1..31 are "HUP", "INT", "QUIT", "ILL", "TRAP", "ABRT", "BUS", "FPE", "KILL", "USR1", "SEGV", "USR2", "PIPE", "ALRM", "TERM", "STKFLT", "CHLD", "CONT", "STOP", "TSTP", "TTIN", "TTOU", "URG", "XCPU", "XFSZ", "VTALRM", "PROF", "WINCH", "IO", "PWR", "SYS"; entry 0 is empty; entries 32..64 are realtime placeholders. NetBSD/FreeBSD callers expect this symbol; some Linux ports (libbsd) provide it. Implementation: same repr(transparent) SysSigList wrapper pattern as the recently-shipped sys_siglist, but holding the abbreviated name strings instead of the descriptions. Tests verify length, well-known indices, and matching shape against sigabbrev_np.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T10:45:13.092404101Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T10:48:48.457995157Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T10:48:48.455973509Z","close_reason":"shipped: sys_signame[NSIG] BSD short signal-name array (HUP/INT/KILL/...) reusing the SysSigList repr(transparent) wrapper + 5 abi tests, all green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-vwrz8","title":"PORT: __pthread_cond_init/destroy/timedwait + __pthread_attr_init/destroy (glibc reserved aliases)","description":"Port the remaining glibc reserved-namespace aliases for the pthread condvar lifecycle + attr lifecycle: __pthread_cond_init, __pthread_cond_destroy, __pthread_cond_timedwait, __pthread_attr_init, __pthread_attr_destroy. These complete the underscored-alias coverage for the condvar and thread-attribute families that libstdc++ thread / std::condition_variable machinery links against. Implementation: thin no_mangle Rust extern "C" wrappers delegating to the public pthread_cond_init/destroy/timedwait and pthread_attr_init/destroy. Tests verify each alias works the same as its public counterpart on a fresh init / destroy round trip and a timed-wait that returns ETIMEDOUT.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T11:42:32.220396231Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T11:42:32.220396231Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Add isolated harness subprocess execution for tests/conformance/fixtures/string_memory_full.json, assert expected_output and host parity by mode, and fix any subprocess-only parity regressions that surface.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AmberSignal","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:12:06.326686709Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T12:25:31.815945052Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T12:25:31.813675668Z","close_reason":"Executed string_memory_full.json through the isolated harness conformance matrix while preserving the existing UB-tolerant host-parity exception; verified locally and via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
